question_text: Which of the following is an example of friction being useful?

options.label: A
options.text: Friction slowing down a car when brakes are applied

options.label: B
options.text: Friction between a ball and the air as it flies through the sky

options.label: C
options.text: Friction between gears in a machine, causing energy loss

options.label: D
options.text: Friction in a door hinge making it harder to open

explanation: Friction is useful when it helps us do things we want, like stopping a car safely with the brakes. The other options show friction being wasteful or unhelpful—air resistance slows objects we want to move, worn gears lose energy, and rusty hinges make doors difficult to open.
